focus: Reducer, state format, convergence references, and tests: documented rigor versus machine-checked guarantees
summary: Deep-review has enforceable shape, severity, blocked-stop, and coarse graph convergence guarantees, but it does not generally machine-check producer/consumer inventory, invariant hypotheses, bug-class saturation, or negative-test search before dimensions count as covered.

title: Dimension coverage can be satisfied by self-reported dimensions rather than bug-class search evidence
summary: Reducer coverage uses iteration dimensions and checked strategy rows, not producer/consumer, invariant, test, or bug-class matrices.

title: Post-dispatch validation enforces field presence and shallow types, not semantic review depth
summary: The validator requires canonical fields and checks selected top-level types, but does not inspect findingDetails contents for scopeProof, affectedSurfaceHints, counterevidence, or bug-class information.

title: findingsNew contract is drift-prone because docs require a severity object but prompt example emits an array
summary: This can let malformed but present JSONL pass validation and weaken convergence accounting.

title: Graph convergence blocks coarse structural gaps but does not model producer, consumer, invariant, test, or bug-class nodes
summary: The review graph currently scores dimensions, findings, P0 resolution, evidence density, and hotspots; deeper bug-discovery artifacts are outside the graph vocabulary.
GraphEvents are optional in both prompt and convergence reference, so graph-assisted stop rigor disappears when iterations omit graph data.
Tests prove blocked-stop surfacing, review graph blockers, and uncovered dimension/file gaps, but not producer/consumer inventory or invariant hypothesis guarantees.
Shallow schema validation allows shallow dimension coverage records to pass.
Graph convergence can help, but only when graphEvents exist and only over the current coarse vocabulary.

reason: Ruled out. It has real blocked-stop, claim-adjudication, severity, evidence-density, and graph-convergence machinery; the problem is that these guarantees mostly operate after findings exist or at stop time, not during candidate generation.
